mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of input buffering in lexical analysis?

options.text: To reduce the number of I/O operations and improve performance
To increase the size of source code that can be compiled
To make the compiler more portable across systems
To enable better error messages

mcqs.explanation: Input buffering primarily reduces frequent I/O operations by reading large chunks of source code into memory, significantly improving compiler performance.

mcqs.question: In the two-buffer scheme, what happens when the forward pointer reaches the end of the first buffer?

options.text: The compiler stops and waits for user input
The second buffer is already loaded and processing continues seamlessly
An error is generated
The first buffer is reloaded immediately

mcqs.explanation: In the two-buffer scheme, while the first buffer is being processed, the second buffer is loaded asynchronously, allowing continuous processing when the pointer switches buffers.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a sentinel in input buffering?

options.text: To mark the beginning of important tokens
To signal buffer boundaries and simplify checking
To indicate comments in the source code
To separate different programming language constructs

mcqs.explanation: A sentinel is a special character placed at the end of each buffer to mark the buffer boundary, simplifying the check for when to switch buffers.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of the double buffering technique?

options.text: Reduced system call overhead
Continuous processing without I/O waits
Simpler implementation than single buffer
Efficient handling of large files

mcqs.explanation: Double buffering is more complex to implement than single buffering due to the need to manage two buffers and coordinate between them.

mcqs.question: How do modern compilers often handle input buffering for optimal performance?

options.text: Using memory-mapped files
Using single buffer with large size
Reading the entire source file into memory
Using multiple small buffers

mcqs.explanation: Many modern compilers use memory-mapped files, which allow the operating system to handle buffer management automatically and provide optimal performance through virtual memory management.
